---
title: Double secret
format: closeread-html
---

Got you! ☜(ﾟヮﾟ☜)

You should watch 

![](./_images/op_logo.png)

And here's why.

::::{.cr-section}


:::{focus-on="cr-mylist"}

First, let's look at the top 3 most popular animation series[^0] according to [IMDB](https://www.imdb.com/search/title/?title_type=tv_series,tv_miniseries&keywords=anime-animation&count=100&interests=in0000026).

:::

:::{#cr-mylist}

We got:

1. Jujutsu Kaisen
2. Attack on Titan
3. One Piece

:::

Jujutsu Kaisen ... is really just Naruto + Demon Slayer
<br>
Yuji = Naruto<br>
Megumi = Sasuke<br>
Nobara = Sakura<br>
Saturo = Kakashi
<br>
and don't try to convince me otherwise.
<br><br>
You can't honestly tell me after watching 5 seasons of Attack on Titan that you were satisified with that ending.
<br><br>
One Piece on the other hand--S tier.

:::{focus-on="cr-filler"}

Oh, but Brittny there's too many episodes. I don't have time.

First off, One Piece has a disgustingly low amount of filler episodes.[^1]

:::

:::{#cr-filler}
```{r}
# Load ggplot2
library(ggplot2)

# Create Data
data <- data.frame(
  group=c('Filler', 'Canon/Mixed'),
  value=c(94, 1061)
)

# Basic piechart
ggplot(data, aes(x="", y=value, fill=group)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity", width=1, color="white") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#2596be", "#ed1b16")) +
  coord_polar("y", start=0) +
  labs(fill="Episode type") +
  theme_void() # remove background, grid, numeric labels
```
:::

Therefore, there's a high percentage of events that are important to the main storyline. What you watch is related to the overall theme and message. Not like Naruto, which has a total of **220** with **90** filler episodes making it **42%** filler.[^2] 

Don't get me wrong I also like Naruto but my point is if you're going to invest so much time into a series, it's worth noting that they're not wasting your time by padding the series unnecessarily.

:::{focus-on="cr-toomany"}

Oh, but Brittny that doesn't help that there's still 1155 episodes and counting (because the series is still ongoing).

:::

Let's do some math here. In this scenario you want to hammer out One Piece as fast as you can so we're only considering canon[^3] episodes. An average episode has a 24 minute run-time. @cr-epmath1

:::{#cr-epmath1}

1,061*24 = 25,464 minutes 

25,464/60 = 424.4 hours

424.4/24 = 17.683 days

That's just over 2.5 weeks--what else are you really doing? 

:::

... but we can be even more realistic about the time.

Normally, an episode starts with an intro and a recap--this can mean the episode really starts like 5 minutes in. Let's also not forget we have an outro and a preview for the next episode which also takes about 5 minutes. So if we're being real about it. That runtime can be cut down by 10 minutes but let's round it down to 8 to be conservative. This can take into account watching each new intro and outro.

Let's redo the math with that in mind, where an average episode would be about 16 minutes in length. @cr-epmath2

:::{#cr-epmath2}

1,061*16 = 16,976 minutes 

25,464/60 = 282.93 hours

424.4/24 = 11.789 days

Now, we're at about 1.5 weeks!

:::

:::{focus-on="cr-commute"}

Here's a scenario. My commute is 1 hour in the morning and 1.5 hours in the evening. This means I could watch 9 episodes during this time.

:::

:::{#cr-commute}
```{r}
# Load ggplot2
# library(ggplot2)

# # Create Data
# data <- data.frame(
#   x=c(seq(9, to=1061, by=9))
# )

# print(head(data, 5))

# # Basic linechart
# ggplot(data, aes(x="", y=value, fill=group)) +
#   geom_bar(stat="identity", width=1, color="white") +
#   scale_fill_manual(values=c("#2596be", "#ed1b16")) +
#   coord_polar("y", start=0) +
#   labs(fill="Episode type") +
#   theme_void() # remove background, grid, numeric labels
```
:::


<!-- ---
title: My First Closeread
format: closeread-html
---

Hello World! Please read my Closeread story below.

:::{.cr-section}

Closeread enables scrollytelling.

Draw your readers attention with: @cr-features

:::{#cr-features}
1. Highlighting
2. Zooming
3. Panning
:::

::: -->

::::

[^0]: Filtering on anime-animation as a keyword
[^1]: [Source](https://www.animefillerlist.com/shows/one-piece)
[^2]: [Source](https://www.animefillerlist.com/shows/naruto)
[^3]: I probably should have noted that canon means that it was in the manga because One Piece started originally in this format.
